title: Footrest for supporting a squatting position
abstract: A footrest designed to support a squatting position during seated- position toilet use. The footrest is comprising two base sections B and B' that are, under normal conditions of use, in contact with a supporting surface and two foot-support sections S and S' adjacent to said base sections. S and S' have an outer surface curved downwards in the longitudinal extension direction towards said base sections B and B'.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A footrest (100) designed to support a squatting position during seated-position toilet use comprising two base sections B (120) and B芒聙聶 (121 ) that are, under normal conditions of use, in contact with a supporting surface (130) and two foot-support sections S (110) and S芒聙聶 (11 1 ) adjacent to said base sections characterized in that S (110) and S芒聙聶 (1 11 ) have an outer surface that is at least partially inclined downwards in the longitudinal extension direction (140) towards said base sections B (120) and B芒聙聶 (121 ). 2. The footrest (100) according to claim 1 , wherein the outer surface of foot-support sections S (1 10) and S芒聙聶 (11 1 ) are entirely inclined downwards in the longitudinal extension direction (140) towards said base sections B (120) and B芒聙聶 (121 ). 3. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above, wherein the downwards inclined surface of the foot-support sections S (110) and S芒聙聶 (11 1 ) have independently from each other in the longitudinal extension direction (140) towards the base sections B (120) or B芒聙聶 (121 ) a maximal longitudinal inclination angle that is between 5脗掳 and 20脗掳, in particular between 8脗掳 and 16脗掳. 4. The footrest (100) according to any one of claims 1 to 2, wherein the downwards inclined outer surfaces of foot-support sections S (110) and S芒聙聶 (1 11 ) are curved downwards in the longitudinal extension direction (140) towards said base sections B (120) and B芒聙聶 (121 ). 5. The footrest (100) according to claim 4, wherein the maximal curvature of the downwards curved outer surfaces of foot-support sections S (1 10) and S芒聙聶 (1 11 ) in the longitudinal extension direction (140) is characterized by an osculating circle with a radius of 0.1cm to 12cm, in particular 1cm to 8cm, more particular 2cm to 7cm. 6. The footrest (100) according to any one of claims 4 and 5, wherein the downwards curved outer surfaces of foot-support sections S (1 10) and S芒聙聶 (1 11 ) in a longitudinal extension direction towards the base sections B (120) or B芒聙聶 (121 ) are independently of each other defined by osculating circles with a decreasing radius with the smallest radius being at the transition from the foot support sections S (110) and S芒聙聶 (1 11 ) to the base sections B (120) or B芒聙聶 (121 ). 7. The footrest (100) according to any one of claims 4 to 6, wherein the downwards curved surface of the foot-support sections S (1 10) and S芒聙聶 (1 11 ) in a longitudinal extension direction (140) towards the base sections B (120) or B芒聙聶 (121 ) is independently from each other defined by a hyperbolic function curve. 8. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above, having in the transverse extension direction (150) a maximal transverse inclination angle of up to 10脗掳, in particular 3脗掳 to 10脗掳, more particular 5脗掳 to 8脗掳. 9. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above, having a length (141 ) at the end of the base sections (125, 126) that are under normal conditions of use in contact with a supporting surface (130) that is shorter than a length at the end of said foot support sections (142) adjacent to said base sections. 10. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above, wherein the base sections B (120) and B芒聙聶 (121 ) have an outer surface that is curved in the transverse extension direction (150). 11. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above, wherein the end of the base sections that are under normal conditions of use in contact with a supporting surface (125, 126) have a width smaller than the maximal width of said footrest. 12. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above, wherein under normal conditions of use the end of the base sections (125, 126) are not in contact with the support surface over the whole width of the base section, but only in two or three sections, in particular two sections. 13. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above comprising an additional connecting section C (160) that links the foot-support sections S (110) and S芒聙聶 (1 11 ). 14. The footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above, wherein said footrest is in a two-part form, wherein the two parts are detachably connected along a division line in the longitudinal extension direction (140). 15. Use of the footrest (100) according to any one of the claims above to support a squatting position during seated-position toilet use.
